comparability_caveat: Rows are qualitative and assumption-sensitive; do not claim JCLS beats Starlink PNT from this table.

rows.clock_correction_assumed: Joint UE and non-reference satellite clock-offset estimation relative to reference satellite
rows.comparability_caveat: Not manuscript-ready; cannot be compared directly to field trials.
rows.gnss_assistance: No GNSS assumed in estimator state
rows.scenario_realism: MIT/Stata UE disk, synthetic Starlink-like LEO geometry, link-budget TOA sigma
rows.signals_used: NTN downlink TOA plus UE sidelink TOA
rows.time_to_solution: Diagnostic; snapshot defaults to 500 ms, time sweep is surrogate only

rows.citation: Kassas et al., first Starlink carrier-phase tracking and positioning results
rows.clock_correction_assumed: Receiver/satellite signal structure handled by opportunistic receiver processing
rows.comparability_caveat: Different signal, receiver, and batching assumptions; do not frame as head-to-head.
rows.gnss_assistance: Often requires known ephemeris/receiver setup assumptions; GNSS-denied motivation
rows.position_error: Reported examples include meter-level to tens-of-meters field results
rows.scenario_realism: Real Starlink signals; not 3GPP NTN PRS
rows.signals_used: Ku-band Starlink signals of opportunity, carrier phase/Doppler style tracking

rows.citation: Differential Doppler LEO opportunistic navigation literature
rows.clock_correction_assumed: Differencing mitigates common errors under baseline assumptions
rows.comparability_caveat: Doppler observable and reference-baseline assumptions differ from JCLS TOA+sidelink.
rows.cooperation: Reference-receiver style differential processing, not UE cooperative JCLS
rows.gnss_assistance: Usually reference receiver, ephemeris, or differential assumptions
rows.signals_used: Doppler or differential Doppler from LEO signals of opportunity

rows.citation: 3GPP NTN overview and Rel-18 positioning discussions
rows.comparability_caveat: Standardization context, not an identical estimator result.
rows.position_error: Not directly comparable; depends on PRS bandwidth, geometry, and timing
rows.signals_used: 5G/NR positioning reference signals and timing measurements

rows.clock_correction_assumed: GNSS or augmentation may supply timing/ephemeris support
rows.comparability_caveat: GNSS assistance changes the ambiguity structure.
rows.signals_used: GNSS plus LEO/NTN augmentation or complementary signals

rows.citation: 5G/6G positioning surveys and integrated NTN positioning work
rows.comparability_caveat: Anchors provide external information that JCLS is trying not to require.
rows.cooperation: Anchor assistance rather than UE cooperation
rows.gnss_assistance: May or may not use GNSS
rows.signals_used: NTN plus terrestrial anchors or integrated terrestrial/non-terrestrial measurements
